qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary command execution via dynamic configuration value evaluation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes command injection via backtick evaluation in the config parser (config.py, globals.py). The advisory describes missing REST API authentication in glances_restful_api.py. Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via unsafe pickle deserialization in version cache
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unsafe pickle deserialization in the version cache (outdated.py). The advisory describes missing REST API authentication in glances_restful_api.py. Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: Neither qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes missing authentication on the Glances REST API (glances_restful_api.py), where the web server binds to 0.0.0.0:61208 without authentication by default. Finding 1 is command injection via backtick evaluation in the config parser (config.py). Finding 2 is RCE via pickle deserialization in the version cache (outdated.py). Both findings target completely different code paths and root causes than the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: glances_restful_api.py lines 259-272 and 477-480 skip authentication when no password is provided
Advisory impact: REST API exposes sensitive system information including process command-lines with credentials
Finding 1 root cause: config.py get_value() evaluates backtick-wrapped values via subprocess.run
Finding 2 root cause: outdated.py uses pickle.load() without integrity verification
No finding references glances_restful_api.py, the missing auth dependency, or the 0.0.0.0 binding behavior
